info.title: Tari Vault
info.description:
Encrypted intermediary for handing off L1 Merkle Proofs to L2 wallets without exposing plaintext proof material to intermediaries.

All vault methods are JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP POST. When bearer-token authentication is enabled on the server, every request must carry an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header — including `rpc.discover`.

methods.name: vault_storeProof
methods.summary: Encrypt and store a proof; return its Claim_ID token.
methods.description:
Encrypts the supplied proof with a freshly generated AES-256-GCM key, stores only the ciphertext, and returns an opaque `Claim_ID` token that encodes both the storage key and the decryption key (key-in-the-ID pattern). The encryption key **never reaches disk**.

Pass the `Claim_ID` through untrusted channels (AI agents, orchestration pipelines, etc.). Only the holder of the `Claim_ID` can decrypt the proof, and only once.

result.name: claim_id
result.description: Opaque 64-character base64url token (no padding). Encodes a 16-byte storage key and a 32-byte AES-256-GCM decryption key. Treat as a secret — anyone who holds it can retrieve the proof.

examples.name: Store a plain-string proof without TTL

value.proof_json: raw proof bytes encoded as a string

methods.name: vault_retrieveProof
methods.summary: Decrypt and consume a stored proof (single-use).
methods.description:
Decodes the `Claim_ID` to recover the storage key and decryption key, fetches and decrypts the ciphertext, **deletes the record** (single-use semantics), and returns the original proof JSON.

A second call with the same `Claim_ID` always returns `ProofNotFound` — the record no longer exists.

If the proof has passed its TTL it is deleted on access and `ProofExpired` is returned.

result.name: proof
result.description: The decrypted proof, wrapped in an object to allow future envelope fields without breaking callers.

methods.name: vault_deleteProof
methods.summary: Abort: delete a stored proof without decrypting it.
methods.description:
Deletes the stored ciphertext without decrypting or returning it. Intended for the **abort / cancel** flow: when a bridge operation is abandoned (L1 burn rolled back, AI agent error, user cancels), the holder of the `Claim_ID` can discard the proof to free storage and prevent stale retrieval attempts.

Requires the full `Claim_ID` — the storage key alone (embedded in the first 22 base64url characters) is not sufficient, preventing an adversary who only knows the storage key from deleting proofs they cannot read.

Returns `ProofNotFound` if the proof has already been retrieved, previously deleted, or never existed.

methods.name: rpc.discover
methods.description:
Standard OpenRPC service-discovery method. Returns the full OpenRPC document describing all methods, schemas, and errors for this API.

**Note:** when bearer-token authentication is enabled, this method also requires the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. The spec is additionally available as `openrpc.json` in the source repository.

result.name: openrpc_document
result.description: The OpenRPC document for this service.

schema.description: A valid OpenRPC 1.2.x document. See https://spec.open-rpc.org/ for the full meta-schema.

ClaimId.title: ClaimId
ClaimId.description: Opaque 64-character base64url (RFC 4648 §5, no padding) token that encodes a 16-byte storage key concatenated with a 32-byte AES-256-GCM encryption key (48 raw bytes total). The decryption key is **never stored server-side** — losing the token means the proof is permanently inaccessible.

proof_json.description: The proof material as any JSON value (object, string, number, array, or boolean). The vault serialises whatever is supplied and encrypts it verbatim — structure is opaque to the vault.

expires_in_secs.description: Time-to-live in seconds from the moment of storage. After the TTL elapses the proof is inaccessible and will be purged by the next cleanup sweep. Omit or set to `null` for a proof that never expires.

ProofObject.title: ProofObject
ProofObject.description: Response body for `vault_retrieveProof`. Wraps the proof in an envelope object so future metadata fields can be added without breaking existing callers.

proof_json.description: The original proof value exactly as it was passed to `vault_storeProof`.
